skill.name: docs-architect
skill.description: Creates comprehensive technical documentation from existing codebases by analyzing architecture, design patterns, components, data flows, and implementation details.
skill.summary: Turns codebase analysis into structured architecture guides, technical manuals, and long-form system documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static findings as false positives. The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary documentation words such as description, design, and described, while the backtick alert is Markdown inline code around a filename reference, not command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Filename Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 26. The line contains Markdown inline code for resources/implementation-playbook.md and does not execute a command or process user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are inside Markdown prose around a documentation path. No shell, Ruby code, script, or command invocation is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3, 4, 6, 36, 43, 48, 55, 64, 70, 72, 81, and 85. The matching text is documentation prose using words such as description, design, and described, with no cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain English documentation guidance. I found no crypto API usage, hashing code, cipher names used as algorithms, or executable implementation.

content.value_statement: Teams often lack clear architecture documentation for complex codebases. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to analyze structure and produce usable technical references.
actual_capabilities: Plans long-form Markdown documentation for existing software systems.
Analyzes code structure, components, dependencies, and integration points.
Organizes architecture guides with executive summaries, sections, appendices, and cross-references.
Documents design decisions, data models, deployment architecture, and security models.
Describes diagrams and flowcharts for architecture, sequence, and process views.
Creates reading paths for developers, architects, and operations teams.
limitations: It depends on the model having access to the relevant codebase files.
It does not generate diagrams as image files by itself.
It can document observed architecture, but it cannot verify undocumented production behavior without evidence.
The referenced resources/implementation-playbook.md file is not present in this package.

use_cases.title: Document a legacy system
use_cases.description: Create a structured technical manual that explains modules, data flows, integrations, and operational concerns.

use_cases.title: Prepare onboarding material
use_cases.description: Produce a reader-friendly architecture guide that helps new developers understand system boundaries and core workflows.

use_cases.title: Support architecture review
use_cases.description: Summarize current design decisions, tradeoffs, risks, and component relationships before a technical review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a codebase overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first documentation pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-architect to review this repository and create a concise architecture overview. Include system purpose, main components, dependencies, and a suggested documentation outline.

prompt_templates.title: Write an onboarding guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for new developer documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-architect to create an onboarding guide for developers joining this project. Explain the architecture, core workflows, key files, and recommended reading path.

prompt_templates.title: Build a technical manual
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for comprehensive system documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-architect to produce a long-form technical manual for this codebase. Include executive summary, architecture overview, components, data models, integrations, deployment, security model, and appendices.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare architecture review material
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request focused on decision support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-architect to prepare architecture review documentation. Identify design decisions, tradeoffs, risks, dependencies, performance considerations, and questions that need stakeholder review.

output_examples.input: Create architecture documentation for a service repository.
output_examples.output: A Markdown manual with an executive summary, component map, integration notes, data model explanations, deployment overview, security model, and glossary.

output_examples.input: Explain a codebase for new developers.
output: Core workflow explanations with file references.

output_examples.output: A review-ready document that captures current architecture, design rationale, known constraints, performance considerations, and open questions.

best_practices: Provide access to representative source files, configuration, and deployment notes before requesting final documentation.
Ask for separate reading paths when the audience includes developers, architects, and operations staff.
Review generated documentation against the codebase before treating it as a source of truth.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to document behavior that is not visible in code, configuration, or supplied context.
Do not use it as a replacement for security, performance, or compliance review.
Do not request a final manual before defining the target audience and required depth.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured technical documentation for software systems, including architecture guides, manuals, onboarding material, and review documents.

faq.question: Does it inspect the codebase directly?
faq.answer: It can analyze files that the active AI tool can access. The quality depends on the available repository context.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams?
faq.answer: It can describe architecture, sequence, and flow diagrams in detail. It does not include a separate image generation tool.

faq.question: Is it suitable for non-developers?
faq.answer: Yes, if you request an executive summary or stakeholder overview. Detailed sections are intended for technical readers.

faq.question: Can it document security architecture?
faq.answer: It can summarize authentication, authorization, and data protection patterns found in the code or supplied context.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the repository, target audience, documentation depth, known architecture decisions, and any operational context.
